title: The 11 Best Live Chat Software
subtitle: A ranked analysis of the top platforms for real-time customer engagement, from simple chat widgets to AI-powered conversational platforms.
audience: Teams adding live chat and chatbots to their site

editor.name: Top 11 Editorial
editor.credential: Autonomous AI ranking engine — methodology v1.0 weights public
editor.conflict_disclosure: None. The editor of Top 11 is not a candidate on this list.

independence.statement: Top 11 takes no payment from any provider on this list. Scores are computed from a public weighted rubric; methodology weights were locked before entry research began.

changelog.text: Initial publication. Methodology v1.0 weights Core Chat Features (25%), Automation & AI (20%), Integrations & API (20%), User Experience (15%), Scalability & Pricing (10%), and Analytics & Reporting (10%).

answer_capsule: The best live chat software is Intercom for its comprehensive conversational platform, followed by LiveChat for dedicated support teams and Zendesk for its integrated help desk suite.

criteria.name: Core Chat Features
criteria.description: Quality of core functionalities: proactive chat, canned responses, chat routing, transcripts, file sharing, and agent collaboration tools.

criteria.name: Automation & AI
criteria.description: Capabilities of the chatbot builder, AI-powered suggestions, automated lead qualification, and out-of-office workflows.

criteria.name: Integrations & API
criteria.description: Breadth and depth of native integrations with CRM, help desk, marketing, and e-commerce platforms, plus API accessibility.

criteria.name: User Experience (UX)
criteria.description: Ease of use for both the support agent in the dashboard and the customer interacting with the chat widget. Includes mobile app quality.

criteria.name: Scalability & Pricing
criteria.description: Fairness and transparency of pricing tiers, value for money, and ability to support a company's growth from startup to enterprise.

criteria.name: Analytics & Reporting
criteria.description: Effectiveness of tools for tracking chat volume, agent performance, response times, and customer satisfaction (CSAT).

solves: integrating chat into a full help desk

guide.q: What is the primary function of live chat software?
guide.a: Live chat software allows businesses to engage with website visitors in real-time through a chat widget. Its primary function is to provide immediate customer support, answer sales questions, and capture leads directly on the website.

guide.q: How does a chatbot differ from live chat?
guide.a: Live chat involves a real human agent talking to a customer. A chatbot is an automated program that simulates human conversation using AI and pre-programmed scripts. Many modern platforms combine both, using chatbots to handle initial queries and routing complex issues to live agents.

how_to_choose: First, define your primary goal: Is it for sales, support, or marketing? Tools like Drift are sales-focused, while LiveChat is support-centric.
Assess your existing tech stack. If you're heavily invested in a CRM like HubSpot or a help desk like Zendesk, their native chat tools offer the tightest integration.
Consider your team's size and budget. Free tools like Tawk.to are great for starting out, while platforms like Intercom are built for teams that can invest in a premium, all-in-one solution.
Evaluate the need for automation. If you want to deflect common questions and qualify leads automatically, prioritize platforms with strong, easy-to-use chatbot builders like Tidio or Freshchat.

faqs.q: How much does live chat software cost?
faqs.a: Pricing varies widely. Some providers like Tawk.to are completely free. Most offer plans starting from $15-$50 per agent per month for basic features. Advanced platforms with AI, chatbots, and marketing automation can cost several hundred to thousands of dollars per month.

faqs.q: Can live chat software increase sales?
faqs.a: Yes, significantly. By engaging proactively with visitors, answering product questions instantly, and resolving issues that might prevent a purchase, live chat can increase conversion rates and reduce cart abandonment, directly boosting sales.

faqs.q: Is live chat software difficult to install?
faqs.a: No, for most platforms, installation is very simple. It typically involves copying and pasting a small snippet of JavaScript code into your website's header or footer. Many also have dedicated plugins for platforms like WordPress, Shopify, and Wix that make it a one-click process.

faqs.q: Do I need a dedicated person to manage live chat?
faqs.a: For the best results, yes. While chatbots can handle off-hours and simple queries, the value of live chat comes from instant human connection. Many small businesses have their sales or support team members manage chat during business hours. As you grow, you may need dedicated chat agents.

honest_disclosures: This ranking favors platforms that offer an 'all-in-one' solution (chat, bots, inbox) over pure-play, single-function tools, as this reflects the current market trend.
Most candidates are US-based SaaS companies, and while their services are global, support hours and documentation may be English-centric.
Pricing for enterprise-level plans is often not public, requiring a sales call. Our `price_max` estimates are based on publicly available data and may not reflect custom enterprise contracts.

glossary.definition: An automated chat invitation that a website sends to a visitor based on predefined triggers, such as time on page, browsing behavior, or referral source. It's used to initiate engagement without the visitor having to click the chat widget first.

entries.name: Intercom
entries.best_for: Businesses seeking a premium, all-in-one platform for conversational sales, marketing, and support.
entries.verdict: Intercom ranks #1 because it offers the most comprehensive and polished platform for managing the entire customer lifecycle through its messenger, combining powerful live chat, sophisticated AI chatbots (Fin), and a shared inbox seamlessly.
entries.verdict_short: The most comprehensive and polished conversational platform for sales, marketing, and support.
entries.praise: Its Fin AI chatbot is best-in-class, providing accurate answers sourced directly from your help content, and the user interface for both agents and customers is exceptionally well-designed.
entries.praise_short: Best-in-class AI chatbot and exceptional user interface.
entries.criticism: The premium features come at a high price, and the pricing structure can be complex and expensive for small businesses or those just starting out.
entries.criticism_short: Complex and expensive pricing, not ideal for startups.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-08.

entries.name: LiveChat
entries.best_for: Dedicated customer support teams of any size who need a powerful, reliable, and easy-to-use live chat solution.
entries.verdict: LiveChat secures the #2 spot by focusing purely on being the best possible live chat tool for support, offering a feature-rich, highly reliable, and intuitive experience for agents and customers alike.
entries.verdict_short: A powerful, reliable, and intuitive pure-play live chat tool for support teams.
entries.praise: The agent application is extremely well-designed, making it easy to handle multiple conversations, and its reporting suite provides deep insights into team performance and customer satisfaction.
entries.praise_short: Excellent agent app and deep performance reporting.
entries.criticism: While it has a separate product, ChatBot, its native AI and automation features are less integrated than all-in-one platforms like Intercom.
entries.criticism_short: AI and chatbot features are less integrated.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-08.

entries.name: Zendesk
entries.best_for: Companies already using the Zendesk ecosystem who want to add a deeply integrated, enterprise-grade chat and messaging solution.
entries.verdict: Zendesk is ranked third due to its unbeatable integration with its own market-leading help desk, providing a unified agent workspace and powerful omnichannel routing for large, complex support operations.
entries.verdict_short: Unbeatable integration with its own help desk for powerful omnichannel support.
entries.praise: Its ability to convert chats into tickets, access customer history, and leverage macros from the Zendesk Suite is second to none for existing users.
entries.praise_short: Seamless chat-to-ticket conversion and unified history.
entries.criticism: As a standalone product, it can feel less intuitive than competitors, and the true power is only unlocked when you commit to the entire, often expensive, Zendesk Suite.
entries.criticism_short: Less intuitive as a standalone; expensive suite required.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-08.

entries.name: HubSpot Service Hub
entries.best_for: Businesses invested in the HubSpot ecosystem that want to connect their chat conversations directly to a powerful, free CRM.
entries.verdict: HubSpot's live chat earns its place for its seamless integration with its free CRM, allowing every conversation to be logged and used for context in future sales, marketing, and service interactions.
entries.verdict_short: Seamlessly integrated with a powerful free CRM for full customer context.
entries.praise: The ability to have a single record for a contact that includes every chat, email, and marketing interaction is incredibly powerful for providing personalized service.
entries.praise_short: Unified contact record across all interactions.
entries.criticism: The core chat features are less advanced than dedicated tools like LiveChat, and the more powerful automation and reporting are locked behind expensive Service Hub tiers.
entries.criticism_short: Core chat features are basic; powerful tools are expensive.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-08.

entries.name: Tidio
entries.best_for: Small to medium-sized businesses, especially in e-commerce, looking for an affordable, easy-to-use platform that combines live chat, chatbots, and email marketing.
entries.verdict: Tidio ranks #5 by offering an exceptional balance of features, ease of use, and affordability, making it the top choice for small businesses that need powerful chat and bot capabilities without a steep learning curve or high cost.
entries.verdict_short: An affordable, easy-to-use chat and bot platform perfect for small businesses.
entries.praise: Its visual chatbot builder is one of the most intuitive on the market, allowing non-technical users to create powerful automation flows with ease.
entries.criticism: While excellent for SMBs, its reporting and user management features may not be robust enough for larger, more complex support organizations.
entries.criticism_short: Reporting and user management are limited for enterprise.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-08.

entries.name: Freshchat (by Freshworks)
entries.best_for: Mid-market and enterprise teams looking for a modern, AI-powered messaging solution that integrates with a broader customer service platform.
entries.verdict: Freshchat stands out for its strong emphasis on AI and bots, offering intelligent routing and AI-powered agent assists that help teams scale their support efforts effectively.
entries.verdict_short: Strong AI and bot capabilities for scaling support teams effectively.
entries.praise: The 'Freddy AI' features provide useful agent assists, smart replies, and a robust chatbot builder that can handle complex customer journeys.
entries.praise_short: Powerful 'Freddy AI' for agent assists and bots.
entries.criticism: The user interface can feel cluttered compared to more streamlined competitors, and like Zendesk, its full potential is best realized within the broader Freshworks ecosystem.
entries.criticism_short: Cluttered UI; best within the Freshworks ecosystem.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-08.

entries.name: Drift
entries.best_for: B2B sales and marketing teams focused on generating qualified leads and booking meetings directly from their website.
entries.verdict: Drift is ranked for pioneering and perfecting the 'conversational marketing' category, with powerful playbooks and account-based marketing (ABM) features designed specifically to engage and convert high-value B2B prospects.
entries.verdict_short: The leader in conversational marketing for engaging high-value B2B prospects.
entries.praise: Its ability to identify target accounts on your site and route them directly to the correct sales rep is a game-changer for B2B sales teams.
entries.praise_short: Excellent for ABM and routing leads to sales.
entries.criticism: Drift is one of the most expensive solutions on the market and is heavily focused on sales use cases, making it a poor fit for teams primarily looking for a customer support tool.
entries.criticism_short: Very expensive and overly focused on sales, not support.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-08.

entries.name: Crisp
entries.best_for: Startups and small businesses looking for a simple, affordable, all-in-one customer messaging platform with a generous free plan.
entries.verdict: Crisp earns its spot by offering a wide array of features—including a shared inbox, CRM, and knowledge base—at an extremely competitive price point, making it a fantastic value proposition for startups.
entries.verdict_short: A feature-rich messaging platform with unbeatable value for startups.
entries.praise: Its flat-rate pricing per workspace, rather than per agent, is highly attractive for growing teams who don't want to be penalized for adding users.
entries.criticism: While it does many things, it lacks the depth and polish in specific areas like AI and advanced reporting compared to more specialized, expensive tools.
entries.criticism_short: Lacks depth in AI and advanced reporting features.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-08.

entries.name: Olark
entries.best_for: Teams that need a simple, reliable, and no-frills live chat tool that is extremely easy to set up and use.
entries.verdict: Olark is ranked for its unwavering focus on simplicity and ease of use, providing a straightforward and effective live chat solution without the complexity of modern all-in-one platforms.
entries.verdict_short: A simple, straightforward, and effective live chat solution without unnecessary complexity.
entries.praise: It's incredibly easy to get started with Olark, and its real-time dashboard and chat tools are intuitive for agents of any technical skill level.
entries.praise_short: Extremely easy to set up and use.
entries.criticism: It significantly lags behind the competition in automation and AI features, lacking any kind of chatbot builder, which is now a standard offering for most providers.
entries.criticism_short: Lacks a chatbot builder and modern automation.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-08.

entries.name: Tawk.to
entries.best_for: Businesses on a tight budget, startups, or anyone who wants a surprisingly robust live chat solution for free.
entries.verdict: Tawk.to makes the list because it offers a genuinely free, feature-rich live chat product with no limits on agents or chat volume, an unbeatable proposition for those with no budget.
entries.verdict_short: A genuinely free and surprisingly feature-rich live chat solution.
entries.praise: For a free tool, it includes an impressive number of features, such as a knowledge base, ticketing, and both desktop and mobile apps.
entries.praise_short: Impressive feature set for a free product.
entries.criticism: The user interface is not as modern or intuitive as paid competitors, and the business model relies on upselling hired chat agents, which can be prominent within the app.
entries.criticism_short: Dated user interface and prominent upsells for agents.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-08.

entries.name: Gist
entries.best_for: Startups and SMBs looking for a HubSpot-like all-in-one platform for marketing, sales, and support at a more accessible price point.
entries.verdict: Our wildcard, Gist, is a hybrid platform that challenges the leaders by bundling live chat, email marketing, a help desk, and more into a single, highly affordable package, making it a contrarian choice for teams who want one tool to do it all.
entries.verdict_short: A highly affordable, bundled platform for teams wanting one tool for everything.
entries.praise: The sheer breadth of functionality offered for the price is outstanding, providing tools that would otherwise require multiple subscriptions.
entries.praise_short: Outstanding breadth of features for the price.
entries.criticism: As a 'jack of all trades,' it's a 'master of none.' The individual tools, including live chat, are not as deep or polished as the best-in-class point solutions.
entries.criticism_short: Individual tools lack the depth of dedicated solutions.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-08.
